title: No respite from heatwave in Odisha despite temperatures dipping below 44°C - Odisha TV
full_text:
Odisha received no relief from the intense summer heat on Thursday despite temperatures dropping marginally across several districts of the State. After days of scorching conditions, the mercury remained below the 44 degrees Celsius mark in most places.

According to weather reports, Bolangir recorded the highest temperature in the State at 43.2 degrees Celsius, followed closely by Sambalpur at 43.1 degrees Celsius and Titlagarh at 43 degrees Celsius. Hirakud registered 42.5 degrees Celsius, while Nuapada recorded 42 degrees Celsius.
Several other western Odisha districts also continued to experience hot and humid weather, with Boudh recording 41.6 degrees Celsius and Bhawanipatna registering 41.5 Degrees Celsius during the day.
In comparison, Cuttack-Bhubaneswar twin cities witnessed relatively comfortable weather conditions. Bhubaneswar recorded a temperature of 37.3 Degrees Celsius, while Cuttack registered 37 Degrees Celsius.
The slight dip in temperature has brought temporary relief for residents who have been struggling with severe heatwave conditions over the past several days. However, hot and humid weather continues to prevail in many parts of the State, especially in interior Odisha.
Weather experts have advised people to remain cautious during afternoon hours and stay hydrated as summer conditions are likely to continue in the coming days despite minor fluctuations in temperature.
In its evening bulletin, the IMD mentioned, “Conditions are favourable for further advance of southwest monsoon into some more parts of southwest and southeast Arabian Sea, Lakshadweep area, southwest, eastcentral and westcentral Bay of Bengal, remaining parts of southeast Bay of Bengal and some parts of northeast Bay of Bengal during the next two to three days. The upper air cyclonic circulation over Northwest Odisha and neighbourhood extending up to 1.5 km above mean sea level has become less marked.”
“People are advised to keep watch on the weather and take safe shelter during thunderstorm activity to protect from lightning and follow traffic advisories in urban areas,” the IMD said.
